name: Ermita de Cuadros - circular from Bedmar
name_original: Ermita de Cuadros - circular desde Bedmar
region_intro: Hiking around Bedmar Y Garcíez offers diverse outdoor opportunities within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park in Jaén, Spain. The region is characterized by rugged limestone mountains, deep river valleys, and unique geological formations. Hikers can explore areas with rich Mediterranean vegetation, precipitous cliffs, and high summits. The landscape includes the significant Cuadros River and the extensive Adelfal de Cuadros. Best hiking trails around Bedmar Y Garcíez The most popular hiking route is Birth of the Cuadros River - circular through the Sierra Mágina natural park, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park and the source of the Cuadros River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ermita de Cuadros - circular from Bedmar, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.0 km) path. This trail offers views of the surrounding Mediterranean landscape and river valleys. Local hikers also love the Source of the Cuadros River – Adelfal de Cuadros Trail loop from Ermita y Torreón de Cuadros, a 2.3 miles (3.8 km) trail leading through the unique Adelfal de Cuadros, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. Hiking around Bedmar Y Garcíez is defined by the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, the Cuadros River, and the extensive oleander forests. The network offers options for easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Bedmar Y Garcíez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Bedmar Y Garcíez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Bedmar y Garcíez?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails in Bedmar y Garcíez, offering a wide range of options from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bedmar y Garcíez?
A: The region offers diverse experiences throughout the year. Spring is particularly beautiful with the cherry trees in bloom, creating a tranquil environment. While high summits like Pico Mágina can be snow-capped in winter, the lower river valleys and oleander forests are generally accessible. Autumn also provides pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors.

Q: What makes hiking in Bedmar y Garcíez unique?
A: Hiking here is defined by the stunning Sierra Mágina Natural Park, characterized by rugged limestone mountains, deep river valleys, and unique geological formations. A standout feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adelfal de Cuadros&lt;/a&gt;, which boasts the largest oleander forest in Europe, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking in Bedmar y Garcíez?
A: Absolutely. Key natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua de Bedmar&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adelfal de Cuadros Trail&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider routes that offer glimpses of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m)&lt;/a&gt;, the highest peak in Jaén province.

Q: Can I visit the Cueva del Agua (Cave of Water) while hiking?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua de Bedmar&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural highlight within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. You can explore it via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua de Bedmar – Cuadros Tunnel loop from Bedmar y Garcíez&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to experience its impressive geological formations.

Q: Where can I experience the unique Adelfal de Cuadros?
A: The Adelfal de Cuadros, home to Europe's largest oleander forest, is a must-see. You can explore this area on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17266573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River – Adelfal de Cuadros Trail loop from Ermita y Torreón de Cuadros&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you directly through this unique natural feature.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Bedmar y Garcíez?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17266573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River – Adelfal de Cuadros Trail loop from Ermita y Torreón de Cuadros&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.8 km route that can be completed in just over an hour, offering a gentle introduction to the area's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Many of the trails in Bedmar y Garcíez are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983145817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birth of the Cuadros River - circular through the Sierra Mágina natural park&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7 km loop that explores the natural park and the source of the Cuadros River.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Bedmar y Garcíez?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from lush river valleys to rugged mountain terrain, and the opportunity to explore unique natural features like the oleander forests and the source of the Cuadros River.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to high viewpoints or summits?
A: Yes, the Sierra Mágina Natural Park offers trails with significant elevation gain leading to impressive viewpoints. For a challenging ascent and panoramic views, consider routes that approach &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive vistas over the Guadalquivir depression.

Q: Is it possible to hike to Pico Mágina, the highest peak in the province?
A: Yes, it is possible to hike to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m)&lt;/a&gt;. A route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31397759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m) – Miramundos Shelter loop from Bélmez de la Moraleda&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging yet rewarding experience to the highest point in Jaén, providing breathtaking panoramic views.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Bedmar River?
A: Yes, the valley of the Bedmar River is another natural space within the municipality. While specific routes are not detailed, the region's rich network of trails often includes paths along riverbanks, offering a different perspective of the local flora and fauna.
tips: The Torreón de Cuadros is a cylindrical tower located in Bedmar y Garcíeza. This tower has a diameter of 6.37 meters and a height of 12 meters, with walls 1.6 meters thick. It was built on an earlier structure, possibly of Moorish origin, and is mentioned in a document by Alfonso X from 1260.
